channel.title: NPR Programs: Morning Edition
channel.description: Morning Edition takes listeners around the country and the world with multi-faceted stories and commentaries every weekday. Hosts Steve Inskeep, Leila Fadel, Michel Martin and A Martínez bring you the latest breaking news and features to prepare you for the day.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Trump says deal to end Iran war has been 'largely negotiated'
item.description: Negotiations to end the war in Iran are "proceeding in an orderly and constructive manner," President Trump wrote Sunday, though he added that he told his negotiators "not to rush into a deal."
item.content:encoded: Negotiations to end the war in Iran are "proceeding in an orderly and constructive manner," President Trump wrote Sunday, though he added that he told his negotiators "not to rush into a deal."
item.dc:creator: Mara Liasson

item.title: Former U.S. ambassador to Israel on pending deal to end Iran war
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to Israel Daniel Shapiro about the Trump administration's pending agreement with Iran to wind down the war in the Middle East.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with former U.S. Ambassador to Israel Daniel Shapiro about the Trump administration's pending agreement with Iran to wind down the war in the Middle East.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Suspected gunman killed after opening fire near White House identified
item.description: Law enforcement officials have released more information about a shooting near the White House on Saturday, including the identity of the alleged gunman.
item.content:encoded: Law enforcement officials have released more information about a shooting near the White House on Saturday, including the identity of the alleged gunman.
item.dc:creator: Chandelis Duster

item.title: Pope Leo takes aim at big tech in sweeping encyclical on AI
item.description: "Magnifica Humanitas" tackles the social, economic and political challenges associated with artificial intelligence.
item.content:encoded:
"Magnifica Humanitas" tackles the social, economic and political challenges associated with artificial intelligence.

(Image credit: Alessandra Tarantino)
item.dc:creator: Claire Giangrave

item.title: Spin to win: How this life insurance company turns healthy habits into a game
item.description: You've heard of lower car insurance rates for good drivers. Now, one life insurance company wants to incentivize longevity with lower rates and extra bonuses for people who adopt healthy habits.
item.content:encoded:
You've heard of lower car insurance rates for good drivers. Now, one life insurance company wants to incentivize longevity with lower rates and extra bonuses for people who adopt healthy habits.

(Image credit: manusapon kasosod/Moment RF)
item.dc:creator: Allison Aubrey

item.title: Morning news brief
item.description: Trump touts breakthrough in negotiations to end Iran war, Middle Eastern countries react to news of a potential deal to end war, Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak.
item.content:encoded: Trump touts breakthrough in negotiations to end Iran war, Middle Eastern countries react to news of a potential deal to end war, Africa races to contain a fast-spreading Ebola outbreak.
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: ABC News' Martha Raddatz talks about her new book, 'The Hero Next Door'
item.description: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with ABC News correspondent Martha Raddatz about her new book, "The Hero Next Door: Stories of Patriotism and Purpose."
item.content:encoded: NPR's Steve Inskeep speaks with ABC News correspondent Martha Raddatz about her new book, "The Hero Next Door: Stories of Patriotism and Purpose."
item.dc:creator: Steve Inskeep

item.title: Congressional Democrats slam Texas candidate over remarks seen as antisemitic
item.description: In the final stretch of a Democratic primary runoff in Texas, the comments of a candidate for the U.S. House have triggered condemnation from local Jewish leaders and national Democrats.
item.content:encoded: In the final stretch of a Democratic primary runoff in Texas, the comments of a candidate for the U.S. House have triggered condemnation from local Jewish leaders and national Democrats.
item.dc:creator: David Martin Davies

item.title: Exploring the evolving relationship between church and state in America
item.description: This week, NPR explores the relationship between church and state through the lens of people who believe the United States is, and should continue to be, a Christian nation.
item.content:encoded: This week, NPR explores the relationship between church and state through the lens of people who believe the United States is, and should continue to be, a Christian nation.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeRose

item.title: Disaster season is coming. Here are three things you can do to prepare
item.description: We look at three things homeowners and renters can do ahead of the summer disaster season to try to ensure any insurance claims they have to file are handled as quickly as possible.
item.content:encoded: We look at three things homeowners and renters can do ahead of the summer disaster season to try to ensure any insurance claims they have to file are handled as quickly as possible.
item.dc:creator: Michael Copley
